content.problemStatement:
Let's see these permutations as arrangements of $N$ items. Initially all the $N$ places are empty and we will go serially and arrange number from $1$ to $N$ in these $N$ places.

First we have to arrange (or place) $1$, currently there are $N$ empty places (or choices).\
Now, we will place $2$, currently there are $N - 1$ empty places (or choices). \
Now, we will place $3$, currently there are $N - 2$ empty places (or choices). \
.\
.\
At last we will place $N$, there will be only $1$ empty place (1 choice).

No matter which place you choose for a particular numbers, the upcoming numbers will have the same number of choices (empty places to choose from). \
Since choices are independent, we will use the rule of product here. 

Number of choices while placing $1$, $N$.\
Number of choices while placing $2$, $N - 1$.\
.\
.\
Number of choices while placing $i$, $N - i + 1$.\
.\
.\
Number of choices while placing $N$, $1$.

So the total number of arrangements will be equal to $N$ $\cdot$ $N - 1$ $\cdot$ $.$ $.$ $2$ $\cdot$ $1$.\
In other words - it is equal to product of all integers from $1$ to $N$. \
This is called $N!$, pronounced as N-factorial.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ â€” denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
